candidates.comparison: async TP compiled (VLLM_COMPILE with fuse_gemm_comms=True) vs standard TP (non-compiled, MP backend)
candidates.c1_weak_oracle: yes — exact dict equality on generated text/token output via compare_two_settings
candidates.c2_realistic_breakage: yes — async TP fuses GEMM+comm ops into different kernels than unfused TP; different numeric paths produce different floating-point results
candidates.c3_no_strong_contract: yes — Not Strong #5: async TP fused distributed compile-pass parity against an unfused baseline. Also Not Strong #2: non-compiled vs compiled mode parity hidden inside compare_two_settings
candidates.code_snippet:
compare_two_settings(
    model_id,
    async_tp_args,
    tp_args,
    method="generate",
    force_v1_runner=True,
)

candidates.comparison: async TP + SP compiled (VLLM_COMPILE with fuse_gemm_comms=True, enable_sp=True) vs standard TP (non-compiled, MP backend) for NVFP4 model
candidates.c1_weak_oracle: yes — exact dict equality on generated text/token output via compare_two_settings
candidates.c2_realistic_breakage: yes — SP + async TP compile pass fuses GEMM+comm and enables sequence parallelism, producing different kernel dispatch and numeric paths than unfused TP
candidates.c3_no_strong_contract: yes — Not Strong #5: sequence parallel + async TP fused distributed compile-pass parity against an unfused baseline
candidates.code_snippet:
compare_two_settings(
    NVFP4_MODEL_ID,
    async_tp_args,
    tp_args,
    method="generate",
    force_v1_runner=True,
)

candidates.comparison: TP with SP compilation (VLLM_COMPILE, enable_sp=True) vs standard TP (non-compiled, MP backend)
candidates.c1_weak_oracle: yes — exact dict equality on generated text/token output via compare_two_settings
candidates.c2_realistic_breakage: yes — SP compile pass changes tensor partitioning and reduction order, producing different floating-point accumulation than non-SP TP
candidates.c3_no_strong_contract: yes — Not Strong #5: sequence parallel compile-pass parity against an unfused baseline. Also Not Strong #2: non-compiled vs compiled mode parity hidden inside compare_two_settings
candidates.code_snippet:
_compare_sp(
    model_id,
    parallel_setup,
    distributed_backend,
    runner,
    test_options,
    num_gpus_available,
    use_inductor_graph_partition,
    fuse_gemm_comms=fuse_gemm_comms,
    enable_prompt_embeds=False,
    method="generate",
    is_multimodal=False,
)

candidates.comparison: TP with SP compilation (VLLM_COMPILE, enable_sp=True, prompt_embeds enabled) vs standard TP (non-compiled, prompt_embeds enabled)
candidates.c1_weak_oracle: yes — exact dict equality on generated text/token output via compare_two_settings
candidates.c2_realistic_breakage: yes — SP compile pass changes tensor partitioning and reduction order; prompt_embeds graph-rewrite path adds another axis of numeric divergence
candidates.c3_no_strong_contract: yes — Not Strong #5: sequence parallel compile-pass parity against an unfused baseline. Both sides use prompt_embeds, so the comparison is still compiled-SP vs non-compiled TP
candidates.code_snippet:
_compare_sp(
    "hmellor/tiny-random-LlamaForCausalLM",
    parallel_setup,
    distributed_backend="mp",
    runner="auto",
    test_options=SPTestOptions(multi_node_only=False, load_format=None),
    num_gpus_available=num_gpus_available,
    use_inductor_graph_partition=use_inductor_graph_partition,
    fuse_gemm_comms=False,
    enable_prompt_embeds=True,
    method="generate",
    is_multimodal=False,
)

candidates.comparison: TP with SP compilation (VLLM_COMPILE, enable_sp=True, fuse_norm_quant=True, fuse_act_quant=True) vs standard TP (non-compiled) for NVFP4 model
candidates.c1_weak_oracle: yes — exact dict equality on generated text/token output via compare_two_settings
candidates.c2_realistic_breakage: yes — SP compile pass with fused norm/act quantization uses entirely different kernel chains than unfused TP; numeric paths diverge under PyTorch/cuBLAS changes
candidates.c3_no_strong_contract: yes — Not Strong #5: sequence parallel fused compile-pass parity against an unfused baseline. Additional fusion (fuse_norm_quant, fuse_act_quant) further separates the compute paths
candidates.code_snippet:
_compare_sp(
    NVFP4_MODEL_ID,
    ParallelSetup(tp_size=2, pp_size=1, fuse_norm_quant=True,
        fuse_act_quant=True, eager_mode=True, chunked_prefill=False),
    "mp",
    "auto",
    SPTestOptions(multi_node_only=False, load_format="dummy",
        model_info=NVFP4_MODEL_INFO),
    num_gpus_available,
    use_inductor_graph_partition=False,
    fuse_gemm_comms=False,
    enable_prompt_embeds=False,
    method="generate",
    is_multimodal=False,
    dtype="bfloat16",
)
